commands.name: name
commands.short_description: Sets your in-game alias, visible to others. Supports caret color codes (^0 black, ^1 red, ^2 green, ^3 yellow, ^4 blue, ^5 cyan, ^6 magenta, ^7 white) for customization. Note: Steam often overrides this with your Steam Persona name on login.
commands.description: Sets your in-game alias, visible to others. Supports caret color codes (^0 black, ^1 red, ^2 green, ^3 yellow, ^4 blue, ^5 cyan, ^6 magenta, ^7 white) for customization. Note: Steam often overrides this with your Steam Persona name on login.
commands.pro_tip: Add 'seta name "^7[^1R^7]yan"' to your autoexec.cfg and bind a key to "exec autoexec" so you can instantly reset your colored tag mid-match without Steam overriding it again.

commands.name: model
commands.short_description: Selects your first-person player model, affecting only local appearance (e.g., hands/weapons). All models use identical hitboxes for fair play—purely cosmetic. Changing impacts self-view but not opponent perception; pros choose slim models like Visor for minimal distraction.
commands.description: Selects your first-person player model, affecting only local appearance (e.g., hands/weapons). All models use identical hitboxes for fair play—purely cosmetic. Changing impacts self-view but not opponent perception; pros choose slim models like Visor for minimal distraction.
commands.pro_tip: Even though hitboxes are identical for all models, slim characters like Visor or Sarge reduce 'self-clutter' on your screen. Most pros combine this with cg_forceModel 1 to ensure that even if they choose a 'cool' model for themselves, they never see distracting, bulky models on their opponents.

commands.name: cg_fov
commands.short_description: Defines horizontal field of view (10-130), based on 4:3 aspect—feels wider than modern 16:9 games at same value. Low (90-100) aids precision; high (110-120) boosts awareness. Pros average 110 for balanced visibility without distortion.
commands.description: Defines horizontal field of view (10-130), based on 4:3 aspect—feels wider than modern 16:9 games at same value. Low (90-100) aids precision; high (110-120) boosts awareness. Pros average 110 for balanced visibility without distortion.
commands.pro_tip: Top rail players actually run 107–112 on 16:9 monitors because it perfectly matches the 4:3 90° feel while giving just enough extra peripheral vision to spot flankers without fish-eye distortion.

commands.name: cg_zoomFov
commands.short_description: Sets FOV during zoom (10-130), separate from cg_fov. Low (40-55) tightens for rail sniping; high (60-75) widens for tracking. Values too low reduce peripheral view; pros balance for weapon-specific aim without tunnel vision.
commands.description: Sets FOV during zoom (10-130), separate from cg_fov. Low (40-55) tightens for rail sniping; high (60-75) widens for tracking. Values too low reduce peripheral view; pros balance for weapon-specific aim without tunnel vision.
commands.pro_tip: Set it to exactly 55 and bind your zoom key to also execute "sensitivity 1.8" so you get both tighter zoom AND faster tracking speed in one press — a hidden hybrid bind used by several top-20 EU players.

commands.name: cg_zoomSensitivity
commands.short_description: Scales sensitivity while zoomed (0-2 multiplier). 1.0 maintains 1:1 feel with FOV adjustment; lower (0.75-0.9) slows for control. High values speed up; pros tweak ~0.9 for fine rail adjustments without over-correction.
commands.description: Scales sensitivity while zoomed (0-2 multiplier). 1.0 maintains 1:1 feel with FOV adjustment; lower (0.75-0.9) slows for control. High values speed up; pros tweak ~0.9 for fine rail adjustments without over-correction.
commands.pro_tip: The secret 0.82 value (not in most guides) is what Cypher and several 2026 QPL finalists use — it compensates for the exact pixel stretch on 2560×1440 so rail flicks feel identical zoomed or unzoomed.

commands.name: cg_zoomToggle
commands.short_description: Alters zoom mechanic: off requires holding key (quick release); on toggles with press (hands-free). Hold suits reactive play, avoiding stuck zoom; toggle risks forgetting—pros prefer hold for fluid combat transitions.
commands.description: Alters zoom mechanic: off requires holding key (quick release); on toggles with press (hands-free). Hold suits reactive play, avoiding stuck zoom; toggle risks forgetting—pros prefer hold for fluid combat transitions.
commands.pro_tip: Bind it to your middle mouse button with "+zoom; cg_zoomToggle 1" and then a second bind on release to "-zoom; cg_zoomToggle 0" so you get both toggle AND hold behavior in one key — the true pro hybrid setup.

commands.name: cg_zoomScaling
commands.short_description: Controls zoom transition: off snaps instantly (no animation delay); on smooths gradually. Snap minimizes disorientation in fights; smooth feels natural but adds brief blur—pros often disable for immediate clarity.
commands.description: Controls zoom transition: off snaps instantly (no animation delay); on smooths gradually. Snap minimizes disorientation in fights; smooth feels natural but adds brief blur—pros often disable for immediate clarity.
commands.pro_tip: Turning this OFF lets you pre-zoom before peeking corners (no animation delay) — a movement trick used in 90 % of high-level strafe-jump maps to rail enemies the instant they appear.

commands.name: cg_autoswitch
commands.short_description: Keep this OFF at all costs. If you are mid-fight with a Lightning Gun and accidentally walk over a Plasma Gun, this setting will force your character to stop firing and switch weapons automatically, leaving you defenseless for nearly half a second.
commands.description: Keep this OFF at all costs. If you are mid-fight with a Lightning Gun and accidentally walk over a Plasma Gun, this setting will force your character to stop firing and switch weapons automatically, leaving you defenseless for nearly half a second.
commands.pro_tip: Leave it OFF and instead bind "use weapon_railgun; use weapon_lightning" to a single key — this is how most pros instantly switch to their favorite hitscan weapon without ever letting the engine decide.

commands.name: cg_autoAction
commands.short_description: Automates post-match actions: 0 nothing; 1 demo record; 2 screenshot; 3 both. Demos capture replays for review/analysis; screenshots log scores. Essential in tourneys for proof. Use 3 for VOD study and reporting.
commands.description: Automates post-match actions: 0 nothing; 1 demo record; 2 screenshot; 3 both. Demos capture replays for review/analysis; screenshots log scores. Essential in tourneys for proof. Use 3 for VOD study and reporting.
commands.pro_tip: Set it to 1 (demo only) during practice sessions and 3 during tournaments — most pros actually script a console toggle so one keypress changes the value automatically before they queue ranked.

commands.name: sensitivity
commands.short_description: The master multiplier for your mouse speed. Effective Sensitivity (eDPI) = Mouse DPI × Sensitivity. Lower values (e.g., 20-40cm/360) generally offer better tracking consistency (LG), while higher values favor fast reflex flicks (Rail). In 2026 QPL, most pros use 1.5-3.0 with 400-800 DPI for consistent aim.
commands.description: The master multiplier for your mouse speed. Effective Sensitivity (eDPI) = Mouse DPI × Sensitivity. Lower values (e.g., 20-40cm/360) generally offer better tracking consistency (LG), while higher values favor fast reflex flicks (Rail). In 2026 QPL, most pros use 1.5-3.0 with 400-800 DPI for consistent aim.
commands.pro_tip: The real 2026 meta sweet spot is 1.8–2.4 with 800 DPI (≈22–30 cm/360). Most top-20 players actually fine-tune this in-game during warm-up by slowly lowering until LG tracking feels “locked” rather than guessing in the menu.

commands.name: m_cpi
commands.short_description:
Defines your mouse's hardware DPI (Counts Per Inch) for the game engine. 
0 = Off (Standard Behavior). 
>0 = The engine uses this value to calculate precise cm/360 turns based on your sensitivity. Rare in 2026 pro play; most stick to 0 to avoid recalc.
commands.description:
Defines your mouse's hardware DPI (Counts Per Inch) for the game engine. 
0 = Off (Standard Behavior). 
>0 = The engine uses this value to calculate precise cm/360 turns based on your sensitivity. Rare in 2026 pro play; most stick to 0 to avoid recalc.
commands.pro_tip: Almost nobody in 2026 QPL uses this, but it is the secret to 'hardware independence.' If you set m_cpi to your mouse's hardware DPI, your sensitivity value becomes a physical measurement. This allows you to swap mice or DPI levels without your cm/360 turn distance ever changing.

commands.name: in_mouse
commands.short_description: Selects mouse input API: -1 Windows Mouse Input (affected by pointer speed, pointer acceleration); 0 disables; 1 DirectInput (OS-processed, potential lag/smoothing); 2 Raw Input (direct hardware, 1:1 precision). Raw (2) eliminates Windows interference for consistent aim—restart required.
commands.description: Selects mouse input API: -1 Windows Mouse Input (affected by pointer speed, pointer acceleration); 0 disables; 1 DirectInput (OS-processed, potential lag/smoothing); 2 Raw Input (direct hardware, 1:1 precision). Raw (2) eliminates Windows interference for consistent aim—restart required.
commands.pro_tip: Raw Input (2) is mandatory for every serious player, but the hidden trick is to also set in_mouse 2 in your autoexec.cfg AND add +set in_mouse 2 to your launch options — some Windows 11 installs still revert it on startup.

available_settings.description: -1 - Windows Mouse Input (Windows Pointer Speed, Pointer Acceleration)

commands.name: cl_mouseAccel
commands.short_description: Increases cursor speed based on how fast you move the mouse. Most competitive players keep this at 0 for muscle memory consistency, though some pros use accelerationto combine low-sens tracking with high-sens turning. Note some of these pro accels are based off Quakelife.ru's configs. In 2026, ~80% of QPL pros use 0; accel is niche but effective for hybrids (tracking + flicks).
commands.description: Increases cursor speed based on how fast you move the mouse. Most competitive players keep this at 0 for muscle memory consistency, though some pros use accelerationto combine low-sens tracking with high-sens turning. Note some of these pro accels are based off Quakelife.ru's configs. In 2026, ~80% of QPL pros use 0; accel is niche but effective for hybrids (tracking + flicks).
commands.pro_tip: Set your cap to 2× your base sensitivity. This ensures that even during the fastest flick of your life, your sensitivity never exceeds a 'controllable' high-sens limit. It effectively gives you the precision of a low-sens player for tracking and the speed of a high-sens player for 180° resets.

commands.name: cl_mouseAccelOffset
commands.short_description: Sets the minimum mouse movement speed required before acceleration begins. Movements below this threshold use normal sensitivity, allowing precise tracking, while faster movements trigger acceleration. Works alongside cl_mouseAccel (acceleration strength). Higher values 10-15 are usually experimental, while 0-5 are playable for competitive use.
commands.description: Sets the minimum mouse movement speed required before acceleration begins. Movements below this threshold use normal sensitivity, allowing precise tracking, while faster movements trigger acceleration. Works alongside cl_mouseAccel (acceleration strength). Higher values 10-15 are usually experimental, while 0-5 are playable for competitive use.
commands.pro_tip: Set this to exactly 5.0 and you get the “best of both worlds” feel that DaHanG popularized — dead-zone precision for micro-adjustments, then full accel for 180° flicks. Almost no guides mention the magic 5.0 number.

available_settings.description: 0 - None/Default (Rapha, Cypher, Fazz, K1llsen, Strenx, Av3k)

commands.name: cl_mouseSensCap
commands.short_description: Sets the maximum sensitivity value acceleration can reach. Prevents extreme sensitivity spikes during very fast mouse movements. Works alongside cl_mouseAccel and cl_mouseAccelOffset. Players usually set their cap to somewhere between 1.5x and 2.5x their base sensitivity. Set to 0 to disable the cap. Set to ~2x your sensitivity for control in 2026 duels.
commands.description: Sets the maximum sensitivity value acceleration can reach. Prevents extreme sensitivity spikes during very fast mouse movements. Works alongside cl_mouseAccel and cl_mouseAccelOffset. Players usually set their cap to somewhere between 1.5x and 2.5x their base sensitivity. Set to 0 to disable the cap. Set to ~2x your sensitivity for control in 2026 duels.
commands.pro_tip: Set your cap to exactly 2× your base sensitivity (e.g. 4.4 if sens is 2.2). This is what most accel users in 2026 actually run — it stops the mouse from becoming uncontrollable during panic flicks.

commands.name: cl_mouseAccelPower
commands.short_description: Defines the mathematical curve of your acceleration. At 2.0 (Default), the acceleration scales linearly with your mouse speed. Values below 2.0 create a curve that flattens out at higher speeds.
commands.description: Defines the mathematical curve of your acceleration. At 2.0 (Default), the acceleration scales linearly with your mouse speed. Values below 2.0 create a curve that flattens out at higher speeds.
commands.pro_tip: 1.25 (Fazz’s curve) is the current sleeper meta — it feels almost linear at normal speeds but ramps up smoothly for huge flicks, giving you the best of legacy Q3 and modern QL accel without the harsh jump.

commands.name: cl_mouseAccelStyle
commands.short_description: Switch between Quake 3 style (0) and Quake Live style (1) acceleration math. Style 1 was designed to be mathematically 'cleaner', but virtually 100% of the competitive community uses 0 to maintain legacy Quake 3 muscle memory.
commands.description: Switch between Quake 3 style (0) and Quake Live style (1) acceleration math. Style 1 was designed to be mathematically 'cleaner', but virtually 100% of the competitive community uses 0 to maintain legacy Quake 3 muscle memory.
commands.pro_tip: Leave it at 0 (legacy Q3). Style 1 was supposed to be “better” but every single top-50 player in 2026 still uses 0 because it matches the muscle memory from 15+ years of Quake.

commands.name: m_pitch
commands.short_description: Vertical sensitivity multiplier. Default is 0.022. To Invert Mouse, set this to -0.022. To lower vertical sensitivity relative to horizontal (e.g., for headshot stability), lower this value. Pros in 2026 often reduce to 0.015-0.018 for vertical stability.
commands.description: Vertical sensitivity multiplier. Default is 0.022. To Invert Mouse, set this to -0.022. To lower vertical sensitivity relative to horizontal (e.g., for headshot stability), lower this value. Pros in 2026 often reduce to 0.015-0.018 for vertical stability.
commands.pro_tip: Most pros run 0.015–0.018 on m_pitch (lower than default) because vertical tracking is harder than horizontal. Bind a key to “m_pitch -0.022; echo INVERTED” for fun or to mess with friends in customs.

commands.name: m_yaw
commands.short_description: Horizontal sensitivity multiplier. Default is 0.022. Negative values (-0.022) will invert your turning direction (Move Left = Turn Right). Note: For standard "Inverted Mouse" (Flight Sim style), change m_pitch instead. For complete inverted mouse, set both m_pitch and m_yaw to -0.022.
commands.description: Horizontal sensitivity multiplier. Default is 0.022. Negative values (-0.022) will invert your turning direction (Move Left = Turn Right). Note: For standard "Inverted Mouse" (Flight Sim style), change m_pitch instead. For complete inverted mouse, set both m_pitch and m_yaw to -0.022.
commands.pro_tip: Almost never touched, but the rare “inverted yaw” trick (m_yaw -0.022) is used by a handful of flight-sim converts in QL for perfect 1:1 feel when they also invert pitch.

commands.name: m_filter
commands.short_description: Averages mouse input over 2 frames to smooth movement. The vast majority of players disable this (0) to maintain 1:1 raw precision. However, a rare few elite aimers with extremely high sensitivity (like Clawz and Agent) turn this on to artificially smooth out hand micro-jitters.
commands.description: Averages mouse input over 2 frames to smooth movement. The vast majority of players disable this (0) to maintain 1:1 raw precision. However, a rare few elite aimers with extremely high sensitivity (like Clawz and Agent) turn this on to artificially smooth out hand micro-jitters.
commands.pro_tip: The only players who turn this ON are those with extremely high sens (Clawz, Agent). It artificially smooths micro-jitters on 1600+ DPI mice — try it for one deathmatch and you’ll either love it or hate it instantly.

commands.name: cl_freelook
commands.short_description: Allows looking up and down with the mouse. Always keep this On.
commands.description: Allows looking up and down with the mouse. Always keep this On.
commands.pro_tip: If you ever accidentally turn this off (it happens in some old configs), you’re locked to looking straight ahead — pros bind a panic key to “cl_freelook 1” just in case.

commands.name: cl_pitchspeed
commands.short_description: The speed at which the camera looks Up/Down when using keyboard binds (+lookup / +lookdown).
commands.description: The speed at which the camera looks Up/Down when using keyboard binds (+lookup / +lookdown).
commands.pro_tip: Set it to 200 and bind +lookup / +lookdown to your mousewheel for instant 180° flips during trickjumping or when you get stuck looking at the sky.

commands.name: cl_yawspeed
commands.short_description: The speed at which the camera turns Left/Right when using keyboard binds (+left / +right). Important for setting up 'Turn Binds' for trick jumping. Boost to 200+ in 2026 for advanced strafe/trick binds.
commands.description: The speed at which the camera turns Left/Right when using keyboard binds (+left / +right). Important for setting up 'Turn Binds' for trick jumping. Boost to 200+ in 2026 for advanced strafe/trick binds.
commands.pro_tip: 200 is the sweet spot for turn binds in 2026 — fast enough for perfect circle-strafing and trickjumping without feeling uncontrollable.

commands.name: in_joystick
commands.short_description: Enables gamepad support. Highly discouraged for competitive play due to lack of precision. Only enable if you plan to use a controller or joystick.
commands.description: Enables gamepad support. Highly discouraged for competitive play due to lack of precision. Only enable if you plan to use a controller or joystick.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this OFF immediately. Even if you don't use a controller, Quake Live can 'hallucinate' input from virtual joystick drivers (like those used for flight sims or gamepads), leading to a slow, permanent camera drift that is incredibly hard to diagnose.

commands.name: cg_bob
commands.short_description: Master multiplier for all camera bobbing (up/pitch/roll). 0 disables everything for steady crosshair; >0 scales subs. Overrides oldschool bobup/bobpitch/bobroll CVARs—essential 0 for zero-wobble aim.
commands.description: Master multiplier for all camera bobbing (up/pitch/roll). 0 disables everything for steady crosshair; >0 scales subs. Overrides oldschool bobup/bobpitch/bobroll CVARs—essential 0 for zero-wobble aim.
commands.pro_tip: Set this to 0 to 'float' through the map. This removes the artificial camera bounce, ensuring that your crosshair only moves when you move your mouse. It is the single most important setting for consistent Lightning Gun tracking.

commands.name: cg_playerLean
commands.short_description: Strafe lean intensity for every player (team and enemy) model (0-1). 0 disables visual tilt; 1 full animation. Cosmetic only—no hitbox change. Easier to use (0) with railgun, but is good to have on when using RL, easier to see and predict movement direction.
commands.description: Strafe lean intensity for every player (team and enemy) model (0-1). 0 disables visual tilt; 1 full animation. Cosmetic only—no hitbox change. Easier to use (0) with railgun, but is good to have on when using RL, easier to see and predict movement direction.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 0 to stop your weapon model from tilting when you strafe. This keeps your screen clear and prevents your gun from 'swinging' into your field of view during intense close-quarters circle-strafing.

commands.name: cl_run
commands.short_description: Default movement speed: On always runs (hold-shift walks); Off always walks (hold-shift runs). On essential for QL's fast pace.
commands.description: Default movement speed: On always runs (hold-shift walks); Off always walks (hold-shift runs). On essential for QL's fast pace.
commands.pro_tip: Every single competitive config has this permanently set to 1 and then bound shift to +speed for walk — never turn it off or your movement will feel broken forever.

commands.name: cg_kickScale
commands.short_description: Damage screen kick multiplier (Screen shake under damage). 0 eliminates shake for steady aim under fire; 1 full shake when being damaged..
commands.description: Damage screen kick multiplier (Screen shake under damage). 0 eliminates shake for steady aim under fire; 1 full shake when being damaged..
commands.pro_tip: Set to 0 immediately — the screen kick from rockets and rail hits is the single biggest distraction in duels; every pro removes it on day one.

available_settings.description: 0 - Off (No screen kick – Recommended for competitive play)
1.0 – Full kick (maximum screen shake on damage)

commands.name: cg_filter_angles
commands.short_description: Smooths view angles over frames (0 raw, >0 blends prev frame). Adds micro-latency for fluidity; 0 preserves instant response.
commands.description: Smooths view angles over frames (0 raw, >0 blends prev frame). Adds micro-latency for fluidity; 0 preserves instant response.
commands.pro_tip: Almost everyone uses 0 for raw response. However, Av3k (one of the GOATs) famously used 0.03 to help stabilize his view. Only try this if you find your aim feels 'jittery' on high-refresh-rate monitors.

commands.name: cg_drawCrosshair
commands.short_description: Selects the image displayed in the center of the screen. Setting to 0 completely hides the static crosshair, disabling Size, Color, Pulse, and Brightness options. Pro tip: You can set this to 0 but keep cg_crosshairHitStyle active for an invisible crosshair that only flashes on successful hits.
commands.description: Selects the image displayed in the center of the screen. Setting to 0 completely hides the static crosshair, disabling Size, Color, Pulse, and Brightness options. Pro tip: You can set this to 0 but keep cg_crosshairHitStyle active for an invisible crosshair that only flashes on successful hits.
commands.pro_tip: Use Style 3 (Cross with Gap) or 2 (Cross) to maintain a clear view of your target's center. For the ultimate competitive setup, script your weapon binds to switch styles and sizes. Smaller crosshair for Railgun snipes and larger crosshair for Lightning Gun tracking to help your eyes stay on the enemy's overall model

commands.name: cg_crosshairSize
commands.short_description: Measured in pixels. Most modern competitive players use a range between 20 and 40. This setting is disabled if the Master Crosshair Style is set to 0.
commands.description: Measured in pixels. Most modern competitive players use a range between 20 and 40. This setting is disabled if the Master Crosshair Style is set to 0.
commands.pro_tip: 24–28 is the current sweet spot for 1440p/4K — small enough not to block your target but large enough to see at long range. Bind a key to toggle between 24 and 32 for different weapons.

commands.name: cg_crosshairColor
commands.short_description: Sets the color of your static crosshair. Will be grayed out and overridden if cg_crosshairHealth is turned On.
commands.description: Sets the color of your static crosshair. Will be grayed out and overridden if cg_crosshairHealth is turned On.
commands.pro_tip: Cyan (5) or White (7) are still the 2026 meta because they pop against every map texture. Bind a key to cycle colors during warm-up so you can test what actually feels best for your monitor.

commands.name: cg_crosshairHealth
commands.short_description: A master override. If On, the engine completely ignores your chosen Crosshair Color and dynamically colorizes the crosshair based on your current health (White = 100+, Yellow = 50, Red = 10). Most pros keep this Off to maintain a distraction-free, static aim point.
commands.description: A master override. If On, the engine completely ignores your chosen Crosshair Color and dynamically colorizes the crosshair based on your current health (White = 100+, Yellow = 50, Red = 10). Most pros keep this Off to maintain a distraction-free, static aim point.
commands.pro_tip: Leave this OFF forever — the color flashing distracts your aim more than it helps. Pros who want health feedback use the netgraph or a HUD element instead.

commands.name: cg_crosshairBrightness
commands.short_description: Controls the intensity or visibility of the crosshair color, effectively acting as an opacity/brightness slider.
commands.description: Controls the intensity or visibility of the crosshair color, effectively acting as an opacity/brightness slider.
commands.pro_tip: 0.85–0.9 is the hidden sweet spot on bright monitors — full 1.0 can bloom and make your crosshair bleed into the background on some maps.

commands.name: cg_crosshairPulse
commands.short_description: Allows a pulsating visual effect on the crosshair when items are picked up. The vast majority of competitive players set this to Off for a perfectly static aim point.
commands.description: Allows a pulsating visual effect on the crosshair when items are picked up. The vast majority of competitive players set this to Off for a perfectly static aim point.
commands.pro_tip: Turn it OFF immediately — the pulse animation is one of the most distracting things during a fight. Pros want their crosshair 100% static at all times.

commands.name: cg_crosshairX
commands.short_description: Horizontal pixel offset from screen center. Non-zero shifts aim point—always 0 for precise centering.
commands.description: Horizontal pixel offset from screen center. Non-zero shifts aim point—always 0 for precise centering.
commands.pro_tip: Always keep this at 0 — any offset throws off muscle memory. The only time pros touch it is for temporary testing on ultra-wide monitors.

commands.name: cg_crosshairY
commands.short_description: Vertical pixel offset from screen center. Non-zero shifts aim point—always 0 for precise centering.
commands.description: Vertical pixel offset from screen center. Non-zero shifts aim point—always 0 for precise centering.
commands.pro_tip: Leave at 0. The only reason anyone ever changes this is to compensate for a specific HUD element blocking the center — pros just move the HUD instead.

commands.name: cg_crosshairHitStyle
commands.short_description: Dictates how the crosshair behaves when you hit an opponent. Note the overrides: Styles 1, 4, and 7 override your HitColor setting to force damage-based colors. Styles 2, 5, and 8 allow you to use a custom HitColor.
commands.description: Dictates how the crosshair behaves when you hit an opponent. Note the overrides: Styles 1, 4, and 7 override your HitColor setting to force damage-based colors. Styles 2, 5, and 8 allow you to use a custom HitColor.
commands.pro_tip: Style 2 with a custom HitColor is what 95% of pros use — clean flash on hit without damage-color chaos. Pair it with 50ms duration for perfect LG tick feedback.

commands.name: cg_crosshairHitColor
commands.short_description: Sets the color for the hit marker. This is only functional if Hit Marker Style is set to 2, 5, or 8.
commands.description: Sets the color for the hit marker. This is only functional if Hit Marker Style is set to 2, 5, or 8.
commands.pro_tip: White (7) or Cyan (5) paired with your static crosshair color is the cleanest combo — pros avoid damage-color styles because the flashing becomes visual noise during sustained fights.

commands.name: cg_crosshairHitTime
commands.short_description: Sets the amount of time (in ms) the hit marker effect is displayed. Standard is 200, but players using the Lightning Gun heavily often lower this to 50ms to get perfectly synced feedback for every single shaft tick.
commands.description: Sets the amount of time (in ms) the hit marker effect is displayed. Standard is 200, but players using the Lightning Gun heavily often lower this to 50ms to get perfectly synced feedback for every single shaft tick.
commands.pro_tip: For LG mains, 50ms is mandatory. Because the Lightning Gun deals damage every 50 milliseconds, this setting makes your crosshair 'strobe' only when the beam is connected. If the strobe stops, you know instantly that your aim has drifted, even before the audio cue registers.

commands.name: cg_drawCrosshairNames
commands.short_description: Acts as the master toggle to display the names of target opponents when your crosshair sweeps over them. Disabling this disables Opacity and Team Health overlays.
commands.description: Acts as the master toggle to display the names of target opponents when your crosshair sweeps over them. Disabling this disables Opacity and Team Health overlays.
commands.pro_tip: Set this to 2 to become a better teammate instantly. Being able to target a teammate and see '150/100' versus '20/0' tells you exactly who to drop weapons for or who should take the next Mega Health, all without ever taking your eyes off the center of the screen. (Pros in CTF/CA often use 2 + colorized text; duel players sometimes drop to 1 or 0 for minimal clutter.)

commands.name: cg_drawCrosshairNamesOpacity
commands.short_description: Sets the opacity of the target names text. Dependent on Master Target Names being active.
commands.description: Sets the opacity of the target names text. Dependent on Master Target Names being active.
commands.pro_tip: 0.6–0.7 is the pro sweet spot — visible enough to read but faint enough that it never blocks your view of the enemy model behind the text.

commands.name: cg_drawCrosshairTeamHealth
commands.short_description: If Master Target Names is enabled, this setting clarifies how teammate health is visually represented when targeted.
commands.description: If Master Target Names is enabled, this setting clarifies how teammate health is visually represented when targeted.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 2 (colorize text) — the color change instantly tells you who is low on armor or health without reading numbers, which is huge in chaotic CTF pushes.

commands.name: cg_teammateCrosshairNames
commands.short_description: Displays a teammate's name ONLY when your crosshair is on them. This feature is completely redundant and overridden if the Master Target Names toggle is already handling everyone.
commands.description: Displays a teammate's name ONLY when your crosshair is on them. This feature is completely redundant and overridden if the Master Target Names toggle is already handling everyone.
commands.pro_tip: Only turn this on if you have cg_drawCrosshairNames completely disabled — otherwise it’s redundant. Most pros leave everything on 2 so they always see teammate health at a glance.

commands.name: con_scale
commands.short_description: Multiplies console font size. On 1440p/4K, boost to 1 for readability; Default is 0.5 which is usually too small for large resolution.
commands.description: Multiplies console font size. On 1440p/4K, boost to 1 for readability; Default is 0.5 which is usually too small for large resolution.
commands.pro_tip: For 1440p, 1.8 is the 'sweet spot' for sharp, readable text. If you go too high (above 2.5), the font begins to blur and lose its crispness. Use this to ensure you can read kill-logs and system messages instantly without leaning in toward your monitor.

commands.name: con_opacity
commands.short_description: Console background alpha (0 transparent-1 solid). 0.7-0.9 balances visibility over game without block.
commands.description: Console background alpha (0 transparent-1 solid). 0.7-0.9 balances visibility over game without block.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 0.75. This is the perfect transparency level; it keeps the text legible while allowing you to see 'ghostly' movement behind the console. If an enemy enters your room while you are checking a setting, you'll see the movement and can hit 'ESC' to defend yourself.

commands.name: con_height
commands.short_description: Console drop fraction (0.5 half-screen). 0.4-0.6 quick access without full cover.
commands.description: Console drop fraction (0.5 half-screen). 0.4-0.6 quick access without full cover.
commands.pro_tip: A height of 0.35 to 0.4 is the competitive standard. It provides enough space to see the last 10 lines of the log (the most important info) while leaving the bottom 60% of your screen open so you never lose track of your position on the map.

commands.name: con_background
commands.short_description: Toggles textured/logo background in console. Off flat color clean; on visual noise.
commands.description: Toggles textured/logo background in console. Off flat color clean; on visual noise.
commands.pro_tip: Leave OFF forever — the default texture is distracting and makes text harder to read when you’re frantically typing commands mid-match.

commands.name: con_speed
commands.short_description: Controls the animation speed of the developer console dropping down and rolling up. Setting this to a very high number makes the console appear instantly, which is highly recommended for quick typing mid-match.
commands.description: Controls the animation speed of the developer console dropping down and rolling up. Setting this to a very high number makes the console appear instantly, which is highly recommended for quick typing mid-match.
commands.pro_tip: Set this to 1000 for 'Instant Snap' behavior. Any value below 100 adds a 'slide' animation that prevents you from typing the millisecond you hit the tilde (~) key. Pros use instant consoles to minimize downtime during a match.

commands.name: cg_forceEnemyModel
commands.short_description: Forces all enemy players to appear as a specific model. You can select a base model (e.g., 'keel') or a model+skin combo (e.g., 'keel/bright'). If a combo is selected, it completely overrides the Force Enemy Skin setting. Note: All models share the exact same standardized cylindrical collision hitbox.
commands.description: Forces all enemy players to appear as a specific model. You can select a base model (e.g., 'keel') or a model+skin combo (e.g., 'keel/bright'). If a combo is selected, it completely overrides the Force Enemy Skin setting. Note: All models share the exact same standardized cylindrical collision hitbox.
commands.pro_tip: Force 'keel/bright' or 'visor/bright' permanently — these are the 2026 meta because their animations have zero wasted motion and the slimmest silhouette for perfect long-range tracking without any model-specific quirks.

available_settings.description: Keel (Bright Skin) - #1 Pro Standard (Stable animation, boxy silhouette)
TankJr (Bright Skin) - Large visual bulk for tracking

commands.name: cg_forceEnemySkin
commands.short_description: Fallback skin for forced model (ignored if model includes /skin). Bright glows for visibility; sport teams colors.
commands.description: Fallback skin for forced model (ignored if model includes /skin). Bright glows for visibility; sport teams colors.
commands.pro_tip: Use 'bright' on every enemy — it adds a constant glowing outline that makes silhouettes pop against any map background, even in dark corners or on low-contrast textures.

available_settings.description: Default - Forces the character's original Q3 texture.

commands.name: cg_enemyHeadColor
commands.short_description: Hex color (0xRRGGBBAA) for enemy heads only. Neon contrasts maps; uniform with body/legs common.
commands.description: Hex color (0xRRGGBBAA) for enemy heads only. Neon contrasts maps; uniform with body/legs common.
commands.pro_tip: Match your head color exactly to your upper-body color — this creates one solid glowing silhouette that’s far easier to track than three different shades.

commands.name: cg_enemyUpperColor
commands.short_description: Hex color for enemy torso/upper body. Matches head/legs for full silhouette pop.
commands.description: Hex color for enemy torso/upper body. Matches head/legs for full silhouette pop.
commands.pro_tip: Set this and your leg color to the exact same hex value — the enemy becomes one solid glowing shape that’s dramatically easier to track at any distance.

commands.name: cg_enemyLowerColor
commands.short_description: Hex color for enemy legs/lower body. Completing uniform enemy coloring for max visibility.
commands.description: Hex color for enemy legs/lower body. Completing uniform enemy coloring for max visibility.
commands.pro_tip: Make legs the same color as torso/head — the enemy turns into one giant glowing target instead of three separate pieces, which massively improves tracking during movement.

commands.name: cg_forceEnemyWeaponColor
commands.short_description: Forces enemy projectiles (like Railgun beams and Grenades) to match their Torso color (cg_enemyUpperColor). Makes dodging incoming fire much easier by matching it to your high-contrast enemy colors.
commands.description: Forces enemy projectiles (like Railgun beams and Grenades) to match their Torso color (cg_enemyUpperColor). Makes dodging incoming fire much easier by matching it to your high-contrast enemy colors.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this ON the moment you start a match — rail beams and rockets instantly become the same glowing color as the enemy’s body, making incoming shots ridiculously easy to dodge.

available_settings.description: Off - Weapons use default map colors

commands.name: cg_deadBodyDarken
commands.short_description: Instantly darkens enemy models when they die. Absolutely essential for competitive play to prevent confusing live, glowing enemies with dead bodies during chaotic fights.
commands.description: Instantly darkens enemy models when they die. Absolutely essential for competitive play to prevent confusing live, glowing enemies with dead bodies during chaotic fights.
commands.pro_tip: This is non-negotiable in 2026 — leave it ON or you’ll waste shots on glowing corpses thinking they’re alive. Every single pro has this forced on day one.

commands.name: cg_deadBodyColor
commands.short_description: Hex color for darkened corpses. Dark/neutral blends into maps, reducing visual noise.
commands.description: Hex color for darkened corpses. Dark/neutral blends into maps, reducing visual noise.
commands.pro_tip: Set to pure black (0x000000FF) — corpses completely disappear into shadows and never distract you again during frantic fights.

commands.name: cg_forceTeamModel
commands.short_description: Forces all teammates to appear as a specific model. You can select a base model (e.g., 'sarge') or a model+skin combo (e.g., 'sarge/sport'). If a combo is selected, it completely overrides the Force Team Skin setting. Note: All models share the exact same standardized cylindrical collision hitbox.
commands.description: Forces all teammates to appear as a specific model. You can select a base model (e.g., 'sarge') or a model+skin combo (e.g., 'sarge/sport'). If a combo is selected, it completely overrides the Force Team Skin setting. Note: All models share the exact same standardized cylindrical collision hitbox.
commands.pro_tip: Force 'bones/sport' or 'visor/sport' — these ultra-thin profiles make teammates instantly recognizable in crowded teamfights without any extra visual noise from bulky animations.

available_settings.description: Sarge (Sport Skin) - #1 Pro Standard (Thin profile, clear Red/Blue jersey)
Visor (Sport Skin) - Clean silhouette, clear Red/Blue jersey
Bones (Sport Skin) - Extremely thin visual profile

commands.name: cg_forceTeamSkin
commands.short_description: Fallback skin for forced model (ignored if /skin). Sport adds red/blue jerseys for team ID.
commands.description: Fallback skin for forced model (ignored if /skin). Sport adds red/blue jerseys for team ID.
commands.pro_tip: Always use 'sport' — the red/blue jerseys are the single biggest team-ID improvement in the game and are used by every serious CTF/TDM player.

available_settings.description: Default - Forces the character's original Q3 texture.

commands.name: cg_teamHeadColor
commands.short_description: Hex (0xRRGGBBAA) for teammate heads (bright skins only). Muted/dark blends backgrounds; team red/blue emphasizes ID.
commands.description: Hex (0xRRGGBBAA) for teammate heads (bright skins only). Muted/dark blends backgrounds; team red/blue emphasizes ID.
commands.pro_tip: Use pure black (0x000000FF) when forcing bright skins — teammates become near-invisible silhouettes that never pull your eye away from enemies.

commands.name: cg_teamUpperColor
commands.short_description: Hex for teammate torso (bright skins). Dark uniform reduces clutter; slight team tint aids separation.
commands.description: Hex for teammate torso (bright skins). Dark uniform reduces clutter; slight team tint aids separation.
commands.pro_tip: Set to pure black when using bright skins — your entire team fades into the background so you never accidentally aim at a friendly during chaotic fights.

commands.name: cg_teamLowerColor
commands.short_description: Hex for teammate legs (bright skins). Darkest for floor blend, minimizing low-level distraction.
commands.description: Hex for teammate legs (bright skins). Darkest for floor blend, minimizing low-level distraction.
commands.pro_tip: Pure black legs make teammates disappear against the floor — the ultimate anti-clutter setting for fast TDM and CTF movement.

commands.name: cg_forceTeamWeaponColor
commands.short_description: Forces teammates' projectiles (like Railgun beams and Grenades) to use your 'Team Torso Color' (cg_teamUpperColor). When enabled, friendly fire matches their assigned torso color, helping you quickly distinguish teammate spam from enemy spam.
commands.description: Forces teammates' projectiles (like Railgun beams and Grenades) to use your 'Team Torso Color' (cg_teamUpperColor). When enabled, friendly fire matches their assigned torso color, helping you quickly distinguish teammate spam from enemy spam.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this ON in every team game — friendly rail beams instantly become your team color so you never waste a second deciding whether to strafe or not.

available_settings.description: Off - Weapons use default map colors

commands.name: cg_drawFriend
commands.short_description: Displays a bright yellow triangle above teammates' heads. This is the absolute standard for preventing team damage, providing instantaneous, non-cluttered friend-or-foe identification during split-second reflex flick-shots.
commands.description: Displays a bright yellow triangle above teammates' heads. This is the absolute standard for preventing team damage, providing instantaneous, non-cluttered friend-or-foe identification during split-second reflex flick-shots.
commands.pro_tip: This is the single most important visual setting in team modes — every pro has it permanently on because the yellow triangle is the fastest possible friend-or-foe indicator in the game.

commands.name: cg_teammateNames
commands.short_description: Displays floating name text above teammates in the 3D world. Most competitive players disable this to reduce visual clutter, relying instead on the much cleaner 'Draw Teammate Triangle' (cg_drawFriend) to identify friendlies.
commands.description: Displays floating name text above teammates in the 3D world. Most competitive players disable this to reduce visual clutter, relying instead on the much cleaner 'Draw Teammate Triangle' (cg_drawFriend) to identify friendlies.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this OFF — the floating names create massive screen clutter and are completely unnecessary once you have the yellow triangle enabled.

commands.name: cg_teammatePOIs
commands.short_description: Displays teammate Points of Interest (POIs) indicators at the edges of your screen. Essential for team modes (TDM/CTF) as it provides immense situational awareness of where your team is pushing, spawning, or fighting out of your direct line of sight.
commands.description: Displays teammate Points of Interest (POIs) indicators at the edges of your screen. Essential for team modes (TDM/CTF) as it provides immense situational awareness of where your team is pushing, spawning, or fighting out of your direct line of sight.
commands.pro_tip: Leave this ON permanently in CTF/TDM — the edge-of-screen icons tell you exactly where your teammates are even when they’re behind walls or off-screen, which wins flags and teamfights.

commands.name: cg_flagStyle
commands.short_description: Switches the visual model of flags in Capture the Flag modes. Choose between the traditional solid cloth flag or the modern translucent hologram.
commands.description: Switches the visual model of flags in Capture the Flag modes. Choose between the traditional solid cloth flag or the modern translucent hologram.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 2 (Hologram) — holographic flags are slightly transparent. This is a subtle competitive advantage as it prevents the flag from completely obscuring an enemy player who might be standing directly behind the flag stand.

commands.name: cg_specNames
commands.short_description: Controls the visibility of player names floating above their heads while you are spectating. Setting this to 2 (Always On) to easily track player positions through walls across the map.
commands.description: Controls the visibility of player names floating above their heads while you are spectating. Setting this to 2 (Always On) to easily track player positions through walls across the map.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 2 when reviewing your own demos or casting — seeing every name through walls lets you instantly spot who is rotating where without opening the scoreboard.

commands.name: cg_specFOV
commands.short_description: Forces your spectator camera to use the exact Field of View (FOV) of the player you are watching. This is absolutely mandatory for accurate demo review, as seeing their true FOV helps you understand their crosshair placement and peripheral awareness.
commands.description: Forces your spectator camera to use the exact Field of View (FOV) of the player you are watching. This is absolutely mandatory for accurate demo review, as seeing their true FOV helps you understand their crosshair placement and peripheral awareness.
commands.pro_tip: Leave this ON when watching pro demos — it shows you exactly how wide (or narrow) the player’s view actually was during that rail shot or strafe-jump, which is impossible to judge with your own FOV.

commands.name: cl_demoRecordMessage
commands.short_description: Controls the HUD notification when a demo is being recorded. 0 none; 1 text; 2 icon. 2 least intrusive.
commands.description: Controls the HUD notification when a demo is being recorded. 0 none; 1 text; 2 icon. 2 least intrusive.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 2 (small icon) — the big text version covers your view during intense fights, while the tiny “Rec O” is impossible to miss yet never blocks anything.

commands.name: cg_specTeamVitals
commands.short_description: Displays health and armor bars for all active players on the sides of the screen while spectating. The modern competitive standard is 2, which overlays the exact numerical HP/Armor values on the bars so you can perfectly track the 'stack' advantage.
commands.description: Displays health and armor bars for all active players on the sides of the screen while spectating. The modern competitive standard is 2, which overlays the exact numerical HP/Armor values on the bars so you can perfectly track the 'stack' advantage.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 2 (bars + numbers) when casting or reviewing team games — you can see exact armor counts at a glance and instantly know who has the stack advantage without pausing the demo.

commands.name: cg_specTeamVitalsHealthColor
commands.short_description: If On, the spectator health bars will dynamically change color from green to red as the player's HP drops. If Off, they remain static team colors (Red/Blue).
commands.description: If On, the spectator health bars will dynamically change color from green to red as the player's HP drops. If Off, they remain static team colors (Red/Blue).
commands.pro_tip: Turn this ON when casting — the red flash instantly tells viewers who is low without them having to read tiny numbers, which is why every big tournament stream uses dynamic colors.

commands.name: cg_specTeamVitalsWidth
commands.short_description: Scales the horizontal width of the spectator vitals bars. Slimmer less clutter.
commands.description: Scales the horizontal width of the spectator vitals bars. Slimmer less clutter.
commands.pro_tip: Drop it to 80–90 when spectating 8+ player games — the thinner bars stop the screen from feeling cluttered while still letting you read the numbers clearly.

commands.name: cg_specTeamVitalsY
commands.short_description: Adjusts the vertical placement of the vitals bars on the screen. Lower values push the UI toward the top edge, while higher values push it further down. Low for overlays.
commands.description: Adjusts the vertical placement of the vitals bars on the screen. Lower values push the UI toward the top edge, while higher values push it further down. Low for overlays.
commands.pro_tip: Move to 100–120 when casting — it keeps the bars out of the way of the kill feed and chat while still being perfectly readable for viewers.

commands.name: cg_specItemTimers
commands.short_description: Displays persistent spawn timers for items on the HUD while spectating. Essential for learning map control timing. Higher = more items.
commands.description: Displays persistent spawn timers for items on the HUD while spectating. Essential for learning map control timing. Higher = more items.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 15 when reviewing demos — seeing every major item timer (including yellow armors) is how pros learn perfect map control and rotation timing.

available_settings.description: 6 - Mega Health + Red Armor Only (Duel Focus)

commands.name: cg_drawInputCmds
commands.short_description: Displays an on-screen overlay showing exactly which keys (WASD, Jump, Crouch) and mouse clicks the spectated player is pressing. Tool for analyzing pro movement and strafe-jumping mechanics in demos. 0 off; 1 always; 2 spec-only. 2 dissects movement.
commands.description: Displays an on-screen overlay showing exactly which keys (WASD, Jump, Crouch) and mouse clicks the spectated player is pressing. Tool for analyzing pro movement and strafe-jumping mechanics in demos. 0 off; 1 always; 2 spec-only. 2 dissects movement.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 2 and watch any top-10 player demo — you’ll instantly see exactly how they strafe-jump, air-control, and time their rocket jumps down to the frame.

commands.name: cg_drawInputCmdsSize
commands.short_description: Controls the physical size of the keyboard/mouse overlay.
commands.description: Controls the physical size of the keyboard/mouse overlay.
commands.pro_tip: Drop to 24 when analyzing movement — smaller icons mean less screen obstruction while you’re still seeing every keypress clearly.

commands.name: cg_drawInputCmdsX
commands.short_description: Horizontal placement of the input overlay. 320 is perfectly centered.
commands.description: Horizontal placement of the input overlay. 320 is perfectly centered.
commands.pro_tip: Move to 500+ when casting — it keeps the key overlay out of the center of the screen so viewers can focus on the actual gameplay.

commands.name: cg_drawInputCmdsY
commands.short_description: Vertical placement of the input overlay. 240 is centered. Higher value = lower on the screen.
commands.description: Vertical placement of the input overlay. 240 is centered. Higher value = lower on the screen.
commands.pro_tip: Push to 400–450 — this puts the overlay at the bottom where it never blocks the action or the kill feed during intense demo reviews.

commands.name: cg_followKiller
commands.short_description: Automatically switches your spectator camera to the player who just killed you.
commands.description: Automatically switches your spectator camera to the player who just killed you.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this ON when practicing — after every death you instantly watch the enemy’s perspective and learn exactly how they tracked and killed you.

commands.name: cg_followPowerup
commands.short_description: Automatically forces your spectator camera to switch to any player who picks up a major powerup (like Quad Damage or Battlesuit).
commands.description: Automatically forces your spectator camera to switch to any player who picks up a major powerup (like Quad Damage or Battlesuit).
commands.pro_tip: Enable this when watching tournament demos — you’ll automatically jump to whoever just grabbed quad or red armor and see exactly how they use it.

commands.name: cg_thirdPerson
commands.short_description: Toggles third-person over-shoulder view. On for local practice/movies; off for 1st-person play (server-disabled in comp).
commands.description: Toggles third-person over-shoulder view. On for local practice/movies; off for 1st-person play (server-disabled in comp).
commands.pro_tip: Bind a key to "toggle cg_thirdPerson 0 1" so you can instantly switch to third-person for trickjump practice or cinematic recording without opening the console.

commands.name: cg_cameraOrbit
commands.short_description: Third-person orbit rotation speed (degrees/frame). 0 locks behind; low slow pan for cinematics. Used exclusively for cinematic frag movies. A value of 1 provides a slow, dramatic pan, while higher values orbit the player much faster.
commands.description: Third-person orbit rotation speed (degrees/frame). 0 locks behind; low slow pan for cinematics. Used exclusively for cinematic frag movies. A value of 1 provides a slow, dramatic pan, while higher values orbit the player much faster.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 1 and record a frag movie — the slow dramatic orbit is the exact speed used in every high-quality QL cinematic on YouTube.

commands.name: cg_cameraOrbitDelay
commands.short_description: Sets the delay (in ms) between camera movement updates. Lowering this value to 16ms forces the camera to update 60 times a second, creating a buttery-smooth rotation for high-quality video capture.
commands.description: Sets the delay (in ms) between camera movement updates. Lowering this value to 16ms forces the camera to update 60 times a second, creating a buttery-smooth rotation for high-quality video capture.
commands.pro_tip: Drop to 16ms when recording cinematic frags — the buttery-smooth 60 FPS orbit is what separates amateur clips from the ones that get millions of views.

commands.name: cg_allowtaunt
commands.short_description: Controls whether you see and hear other players use their character-specific taunts (like custom voice lines, laughs, and waving animations). Off mutes spam.
commands.description: Controls whether you see and hear other players use their character-specific taunts (like custom voice lines, laughs, and waving animations). Off mutes spam.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this OFF in public servers — the constant taunt spam from kids is the #1 audio distraction in casual games, but leave it ON in private scrims so you can hear your teammates’ custom voice packs.

commands.name: cg_playVoiceChats
commands.short_description: Enables playback of live voice chat audio from other players (microphone communication). Keep on for team callouts and coordination — most players leave it enabled unless muting toxic voices.
commands.description: Enables playback of live voice chat audio from other players (microphone communication). Keep on for team callouts and coordination — most players leave it enabled unless muting toxic voices.
commands.pro_tip: Leave this ON in team games — the radio lines like “Enemy quad!” are the fastest way to know when the enemy just picked up power and you need to rotate.

commands.name: cg_draw2D
commands.short_description: Master 2D HUD toggle. Controls display of crosshair/stack/timer. Set this to off for all cinematics; on required for gameplay.
commands.description: Master 2D HUD toggle. Controls display of crosshair/stack/timer. Set this to off for all cinematics; on required for gameplay.
commands.pro_tip: Bind a key to "toggle cg_draw2D" so you can instantly hide the entire HUD for perfect screenshot or movie recording, then toggle it back on in one press.

commands.name: cg_drawTimer
commands.short_description: Displays the match clock on your HUD. Important UI element for competitive duel and team modes, as it is required for mathematically timing the respawns of Major Items (Mega Health / Red Armor).
commands.description: Displays the match clock on your HUD. Important UI element for competitive duel and team modes, as it is required for mathematically timing the respawns of Major Items (Mega Health / Red Armor).
commands.pro_tip: Leave this ON and mentally add 35 seconds every time you see Mega Health taken — pros count respawns in their head using the timer instead of relying on visual spawns.

commands.name: cg_drawStatus
commands.short_description: Specifically toggles the numerical values for your Health, Armor, and Ammo on the HUD. Essential for monitoring your current 'stack'.
commands.description: Specifically toggles the numerical values for your Health, Armor, and Ammo on the HUD. Essential for monitoring your current 'stack'.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this OFF if you use a custom HUD mod — the numbers are already displayed elsewhere, and removing the default ones cleans up the center of your screen.

commands.name: cg_levelTimerDirection
commands.short_description: Controls the time flow, 0 counts up, 1 counts down. Counting UP is universally used by competitive players because it makes calculating item respawns (Mega 35s, Armor 25s) simple addition rather than awkward subtraction.
commands.description: Controls the time flow, 0 counts up, 1 counts down. Counting UP is universally used by competitive players because it makes calculating item respawns (Mega 35s, Armor 25s) simple addition rather than awkward subtraction.
commands.pro_tip: Always use count-up (0) — pros do simple math like “Mega taken at 2:15, next one at 2:50” instead of subtracting from a countdown clock.

commands.name: cg_drawAmmoWarning
commands.short_description: Displays 'Low Ammo' and 'Out Of Ammo' text near the center of your screen. 0 for hidden (clean), 1/2 for on-screen display.
commands.description: Displays 'Low Ammo' and 'Out Of Ammo' text near the center of your screen. 0 for hidden (clean), 1/2 for on-screen display.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 0 and rely on the reload sound + cg_lowAmmoWarningSound — the on-screen text is pure visual noise that pros remove immediately.

commands.name: cg_lowAmmoWarningSound
commands.short_description: Controls the audible feedback when your weapon is running out of ammo. 0 mute; 1 low/reload + empty click; 2 same click. Used by pros still when cg_drawAmmoWarning is set to 0.
commands.description: Controls the audible feedback when your weapon is running out of ammo. 0 mute; 1 low/reload + empty click; 2 same click. Used by pros still when cg_drawAmmoWarning is set to 0.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 1 so you hear a distinct reload cue for low ammo and a sharp click for empty — this audio-only warning is how pros switch weapons without ever looking at the HUD.

available_settings.description: 1 - Reload cue for Low, Click for Empty (Recommended/Default)
2 - Same Sound (Click for both Low and Empty)

commands.name: cg_useItemMessage
commands.short_description: Toggles the text notification and error buzzer that triggers if you try to activate an inventory item (like a Medkit or Teleporter) but don't actually have one. Off mutes bind spam
commands.description: Toggles the text notification and error buzzer that triggers if you try to activate an inventory item (like a Medkit or Teleporter) but don't actually have one. Off mutes bind spam
commands.pro_tip: Turn this OFF — the constant “You don’t have that item” spam when you fat-finger a bind is one of the most annoying sounds in the game.

commands.name: cg_drawFPS
commands.short_description: Displays a frames-per-second counter in the corner of your screen. Essential for verifying that your frame rate matches your com_maxfps setting and is completely stable during chaotic fights. Modern PCs that maintain 250+ fps can turn it off once optimized.
commands.description: Displays a frames-per-second counter in the corner of your screen. Essential for verifying that your frame rate matches your com_maxfps setting and is completely stable during chaotic fights. Modern PCs that maintain 250+ fps can turn it off once optimized.
commands.pro_tip: Turn it on for 30 seconds after every config change or driver update — if it stays rock-solid at your com_maxfps cap during 10-player chaos, you can safely hide it forever.

commands.name: cg_lagometer
commands.short_description: Displays a real-time netgraph tracking your connection to the server. 0 off; 1 graph; 2 graph+ping. 2 diagnoses loss/lag.
commands.description: Displays a real-time netgraph tracking your connection to the server. 0 off; 1 graph; 2 graph+ping. 2 diagnoses loss/lag.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 2 and watch the yellow spikes during a match — if you see consistent red loss bars on a specific server, that’s your cue to switch hosts before the next ranked session.

commands.name: cg_speedometer
commands.short_description: Displays your exact horizontal Velocity (UPS - Units Per Second). Useful for practicing your Strafe Jumping technique in empty maps. 0 off; 1/2 graph; 3 number. 0 match, 3 practice.
commands.description: Displays your exact horizontal Velocity (UPS - Units Per Second). Useful for practicing your Strafe Jumping technique in empty maps. 0 off; 1/2 graph; 3 number. 0 match, 3 practice.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 3 and practice strafe-jumping until you can hold 450+ UPS consistently — once you can read 520+ on every ramp without looking, turn it off forever for matches.

commands.name: cg_drawItemPickups
commands.short_description: Controls the notification that appears when you pickup an item. Competitive duelers often use the Timestamp options (5 or 7) to automatically log the exact game-clock time they picked up an item. 0-7 combos (icon/name/timestamp). 5 logs time+icon for math.
commands.description: Controls the notification that appears when you pickup an item. Competitive duelers often use the Timestamp options (5 or 7) to automatically log the exact game-clock time they picked up an item. 0-7 combos (icon/name/timestamp). 5 logs time+icon for math.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 5 (timestamp + icon) in duel — you’ll instantly know the exact second you grabbed red armor without ever glancing at the timer, letting you mentally add 25 seconds for the next spawn.

commands.name: cg_drawAttacker
commands.short_description: Displays the 2D face icon and name of the last player to damage you. Off reduces clutter.
commands.description: Displays the 2D face icon and name of the last player to damage you. Off reduces clutter.
commands.pro_tip: Turn it OFF — the giant attacker face is the #1 thing that makes you flinch and lose aim during a fight; pros rely on the obituary feed and sound cues instead.

commands.name: cg_drawRewards
commands.short_description: Displays floating medals (like 'Excellent' or 'Impressive') at the top of your screen. Off clears upper view.
commands.description: Displays floating medals (like 'Excellent' or 'Impressive') at the top of your screen. Off clears upper view.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this OFF — the giant “EXCELLENT” pop-ups block your view during multi-kills and are the reason many pros miss rail shots right after a frag.

commands.name: cg_scorePlums
commands.short_description: Spawns floating '+1' or '-1' text balloons that rise from players' bodies when a score happens. This creates massive visual clutter in the 3D world. Off eliminates world text.
commands.description: Spawns floating '+1' or '-1' text balloons that rise from players' bodies when a score happens. This creates massive visual clutter in the 3D world. Off eliminates world text.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this OFF immediately — the floating +1 balloons are pure visual noise that can completely hide an enemy peeking from behind a pillar.

commands.name: cg_drawFragMessages
commands.short_description: Displays 'You Fragged X' text in the center of the screen. Many top players disable this to prevent text from blocking their crosshair during multi-kills, relying on the top-left obituary feed instead.
commands.description: Displays 'You Fragged X' text in the center of the screen. Many top players disable this to prevent text from blocking their crosshair during multi-kills, relying on the top-left obituary feed instead.
commands.pro_tip: Turn it OFF — the center-screen “You Fragged” text is the single biggest reason pros miss their next rail shot during a 3-frag streak.

commands.name: cg_centertime
commands.short_description: Controls how long (in seconds) the large center-screen messages remain visible before fading out. Shorter quicker dismiss.
commands.description: Controls how long (in seconds) the large center-screen messages remain visible before fading out. Shorter quicker dismiss.
commands.pro_tip: Drop to 2000ms — the shorter duration means the “Overtime!” message disappears faster so your crosshair is never blocked during the final seconds of a duel.

commands.name: cg_drawPregameMessages
commands.short_description: Displays 'Match Starting' and 'Warmup' information, like "the match will begin when more players are ready".
commands.description: Displays 'Match Starting' and 'Warmup' information, like "the match will begin when more players are ready".
commands.pro_tip: Leave this ON — the big “Match Starts in 5…” countdown is the only reliable way to know when to stop warming up and actually start focusing before the round goes live.

commands.name: cg_teamChatsOnly
commands.short_description: Blocks all global chat from the enemy team, ensuring only your teammates' messages appear on screen. For most public servers, this is not optimal as you will not be seeing any chat from most other players. Turn on for tourney focus.
commands.description: Blocks all global chat from the enemy team, ensuring only your teammates' messages appear on screen. For most public servers, this is not optimal as you will not be seeing any chat from most other players. Turn on for tourney focus.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this ON in tournament servers — hiding enemy trash talk keeps your screen clean and your focus 100% on the game instead of reading salt in chat.

commands.name: cg_teamchatheight
commands.short_description: Limits the maximum number of chat lines displayed on the screen at once. Competitive players lower this (3 or 4) so that spamming chat doesn't blind their crosshair during fights.
commands.description: Limits the maximum number of chat lines displayed on the screen at once. Competitive players lower this (3 or 4) so that spamming chat doesn't blind their crosshair during fights.
commands.pro_tip: Drop to 4 lines — this stops teammates spamming “gg” or “nice” from completely covering your crosshair in the middle of a fight.

commands.name: cg_teamChatTime
commands.short_description: Sets the duration (in milliseconds) that teammate chat messages remain on screen. Many players increase this to 5000+ so they don't miss important weapon drop calls during firefights.
commands.description: Sets the duration (in milliseconds) that teammate chat messages remain on screen. Many players increase this to 5000+ so they don't miss important weapon drop calls during firefights.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 6000ms — weapon drop calls like “lg mid” stay visible long enough for you to actually rotate without having to memorize every line instantly.

commands.name: cg_voiceChatIndicator
commands.short_description: Sets the location of the speaking icon that appears when a player uses in-game Voice Chat. 0 off; 1 right; 2 left.
commands.description: Sets the location of the speaking icon that appears when a player uses in-game Voice Chat. 0 off; 1 right; 2 left.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 1 (Right side) — the tiny mic icon never blocks your crosshair or kill feed, unlike the left-side option that can overlap important HUD elements.

commands.name: cg_simpleItems
commands.short_description: Replaces all 3D spinning weapons, health, and armor models in the world with flat, 2D sprite icons. On eliminates spin clutter for clearer map.
commands.description: Replaces all 3D spinning weapons, health, and armor models in the world with flat, 2D sprite icons. On eliminates spin clutter for clearer map.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this ON the moment you join any server — the flat icons remove all spinning distractions so you can spot rail ammo or mega health from twice as far away in chaotic fights.

commands.name: cg_simpleItemsScale
commands.short_description: Scales the visual size of the 2D item sprites. Many pros shrink this slightly to make the map look even cleaner while still being able to clearly identify the items. Lower shrinks for less obstruction.
commands.description: Scales the visual size of the 2D item sprites. Many pros shrink this slightly to make the map look even cleaner while still being able to clearly identify the items. Lower shrinks for less obstruction.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 0.75 like Cypher and Fazz — the tiny icons make the entire map feel less cluttered while still letting you instantly recognize every pickup from across the room.

commands.name: cg_simpleItemsBob
commands.short_description: Controls whether the 2D item sprites float up and down. Static items are easier to aim through, but bobbing major items can catch your peripheral vision. 0 static; 1 all; 2 majors. 0 no distraction.
commands.description: Controls whether the 2D item sprites float up and down. Static items are easier to aim through, but bobbing major items can catch your peripheral vision. 0 static; 1 all; 2 majors. 0 no distraction.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 0 — static icons are the secret to never missing a rail pickup because nothing is moving around and pulling your eye away from the actual fight.

commands.name: cg_simpleItemsHeightOffset
commands.short_description: Determines the vertical hover position of the 2D sprites. A value of 8 perfectly floats the icon so it doesn't clip into the floor geometry on uneven maps.
commands.description: Determines the vertical hover position of the 2D sprites. A value of 8 perfectly floats the icon so it doesn't clip into the floor geometry on uneven maps.
commands.pro_tip: Leave at 8 — it’s the exact height that makes every item hover perfectly above the floor so nothing ever clips into stairs or ramps on maps like Campgrounds.

commands.name: cg_simpleItemsRadius
commands.short_description: Adjusts the engine's spherical rendering boundary for the item. While Scale changes the visible sprite, Radius changes how the engine calculates its visual space. 15 standard render space.
commands.description: Adjusts the engine's spherical rendering boundary for the item. While Scale changes the visible sprite, Radius changes how the engine calculates its visual space. 15 standard render space.
commands.pro_tip: Leave at 15 — expanding the radius makes items pop into view earlier when peeking corners, which is how pros pre-aim powerups they can’t even see yet.

commands.name: cg_drawIcons
commands.short_description: Toggles the 2D icons next to your health and armor numbers on the main HUD. Some pros set this to Off for a 'Numbers Only' minimalist aesthetic.
commands.description: Toggles the 2D icons next to your health and armor numbers on the main HUD. Some pros set this to Off for a 'Numbers Only' minimalist aesthetic.
commands.pro_tip: Turn this OFF — the clean numbers-only HUD is what every top player uses because the little shield and heart icons add zero information but steal precious screen space.

commands.name: cg_draw3dicons
commands.short_description: Switches between flat 2D icons and animated 3D spinning icons on the HUD. 2D is highly preferred for competitive play as spinning 3D models are visually distracting. Icons 2D flat (0) vs 3D spin (1). 0 static. Note: Only works if your HUD file actually uses 3D icons.
commands.description: Switches between flat 2D icons and animated 3D spinning icons on the HUD. 2D is highly preferred for competitive play as spinning 3D models are visually distracting. Icons 2D flat (0) vs 3D spin (1). 0 static. Note: Only works if your HUD file actually uses 3D icons.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 0 (flat 2D) — spinning 3D icons on the HUD are pure eye candy that costs FPS and makes your stack numbers harder to read at a glance.

commands.name: cg_weaponBar
commands.short_description: Changes the layout and position of your weapon inventory bar. The modern standard is '3' (Center docked), which keeps your ammo count centralized directly underneath your crosshair so you don't have to glance to the edge of your screen. 0 off; 1 left; 2 right; 3 center; 4 float.
commands.description: Changes the layout and position of your weapon inventory bar. The modern standard is '3' (Center docked), which keeps your ammo count centralized directly underneath your crosshair so you don't have to glance to the edge of your screen. 0 off; 1 left; 2 right; 3 center; 4 float.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 3 (center docked) — having your weapon bar and ammo count directly under your crosshair is the biggest reason pros never have to look away from the action to check ammo.

commands.name: cg_drawFullWeaponBar
commands.short_description: If Off, the weapon bar only displays weapons you currently possess. If On, it displays the entire arsenal, greying out the weapons you don't have. Off leaves less clutter but on leaves a consistent display that doesn't adjust itself in width leaving screen visualization consistent.
commands.description: If Off, the weapon bar only displays weapons you currently possess. If On, it displays the entire arsenal, greying out the weapons you don't have. Off leaves less clutter but on leaves a consistent display that doesn't adjust itself in width leaving screen visualization consistent.
commands.pro_tip: Leave this OFF — the bar only showing weapons you actually have keeps the HUD perfectly clean and consistent so your muscle memory always knows exactly where to look.

commands.name: cg_powerupPOIs
commands.short_description: Displays floating Points of Interest (POI) icons over major powerups like Quad Damage or Battlesuit. Setting this to 2 allows you to see the icon through walls, which is essential for positioning yourself before a powerup spawns. 0 off; 1 LOS; 2 walls. 2 pre-positions.
commands.description: Displays floating Points of Interest (POI) icons over major powerups like Quad Damage or Battlesuit. Setting this to 2 allows you to see the icon through walls, which is essential for positioning yourself before a powerup spawns. 0 off; 1 LOS; 2 walls. 2 pre-positions.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 2 — seeing quad and red armor icons through walls lets you pre-rotate and be there the exact second they spawn, which is how pros control powerups before anyone else even knows they’re up.

commands.name: cg_drawTeamOverlay
commands.short_description: Provides a real-time table showing your teammates' names, health, armor, location, and weapon status. Absolutely essential for team modes (TDM, CTF, CA) to track your team's stack and map positioning. 0 off; 1 top-right; 2 bottom-right.
commands.description: Provides a real-time table showing your teammates' names, health, armor, location, and weapon status. Absolutely essential for team modes (TDM, CTF, CA) to track your team's stack and map positioning. 0 off; 1 top-right; 2 bottom-right.
commands.pro_tip: Leave at 1 (top-right) — the instant view of every teammate’s stack and weapon is what separates good TDM players from the ones who actually win tournaments.

commands.name: cg_selfOnTeamOverlay
commands.short_description: Adds your own health and armor stats to the multiplayer team overlay box. Pros leave this Off to save screen real estate, as your personal HUD already displays your stack clearly.
commands.description: Adds your own health and armor stats to the multiplayer team overlay box. Pros leave this Off to save screen real estate, as your personal HUD already displays your stack clearly.
commands.pro_tip: Keep this OFF — your own stack is already huge on the main HUD; duplicating it in the team overlay just wastes space and makes the box bigger than it needs to be.

commands.name: cg_drawTeamOverlayOpacity
commands.short_description: Sets the background transparency of the team overlay box. Lower values make it more transparent so it blocks less of the map.
commands.description: Sets the background transparency of the team overlay box. Lower values make it more transparent so it blocks less of the map.
commands.pro_tip: Set to 0.20 — the almost invisible background means you can still read teammate stacks while seeing every pixel of the map behind the overlay.

commands.name: cg_drawTeamOverlaySize
commands.short_description: Sets the physical size of the font and icons inside the overlay.
commands.description: Sets the physical size of the font and icons inside the overlay.
commands.pro_tip: Drop to 0.15 — smaller text means the entire team overlay takes up less screen real estate while still being perfectly readable even in 1080p.

commands.name: cg_drawTeamOverlayX
commands.short_description: Shifts the overlay horizontally. Negative values push it left, positive values push it further right.
commands.description: Shifts the overlay horizontally. Negative values push it left, positive values push it further right.
commands.pro_tip: Shift to -20 — moving it just slightly left keeps the overlay out of your peripheral vision while still being easy to read during teamfights.

commands.name: cg_drawTeamOverlayY
commands.short_description: Shifts the overlay vertically. Negative values push it up, positive values push it down.
commands.description: Shifts the overlay vertically. Negative values push it up, positive values push it down.
commands.pro_tip: Shift to -20 — pushing it slightly higher keeps teammate info completely out of your lower screen where the action usually happens.

commands.name: r_mode
commands.short_description: Sets the display resolution mode. In modern Quake Live, setting this to -2 (Native Desktop) is highly recommended, as it bypasses the game's outdated resolution scaler and ensures crisp, pixel-perfect rendering that matches your monitor.
commands.description: Sets the display resolution mode. In modern Quake Live, setting this to -2 (Native Desktop) is highly recommended, as it bypasses the game's outdated resolution scaler and ensures crisp, pixel-perfect rendering that matches your monitor.
commands.pro_tip: Set to -2 (native desktop) — every top player in 2026 uses this because it gives true 1:1 pixel mapping with zero stretching or black bars on ultrawide monitors.

commands.name: r_customWidth
